sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
OK, looking at the current state:
Row 38: ccggggNNccc (cols 8-11 are g, 12-13 are N)
Row 39: ccggggNNccc (cols 8-11 are g, 12-13 are N)
The green square is at cols 12-13. When I tried RIGHT again, the board changed but the green square stayed at cols 12-13. So it's blocked from moving further right.
Looking at the layout:
- The gray container walls are at cols 8-11 (rows 38-39) and cols 8-13 (rows 40-43)
- The green square is at cols 12-13 (rows 38-39)
- To the right of the green square is charcoal (c) background
Wait, but cols 12-13 were previously gray walls. Now the green square is there. And when I try to move right again, it doesn't move. So there's a barrier at col 14.
Let me look more carefully. The ASCII shows:
Row 38: ccggggNNccc
That's cols 6-16: c, c, g, g, g, g, N, N, c, c, c
So col 6=c, 7=c, 8=g, 9=g, 10=g, 11=g, 12=N, 13=N, 14=c, 15=c, 16=c
The green square is at cols 12-13, and col 14 is charcoal (background). So the green square is at the edge of the container, and there's charcoal to the right.
But the green square can't move right. Why? Maybe there's an invisible barrier, or maybe the container's boundary prevents it.
Wait, actually the container's right wall was at col 13. The green square is now at cols 12-13, which means it's partially outside the original container boundary. But it can't go further right.
Hmm, let me reconsider. Maybe the green square can only move within the container's original bounds. The container is cols 8-13, rows 38-43. The green square is 2x2, so its right edge is at col 13, which is the container's right boundary.
So the green square can move within the container but not outside it. The container walls at rows 38-39, cols 12-13 were "soft" - the green square can occupy those cells. But the container's right boundary at col 13 is still a hard limit.
OK so the green square is now at the rightmost position within the container (cols 12-13). Let me try moving it UP to see if it can escape through the top.
Actually wait, I already tried UP before and it didn't work. The green square was at rows 38-39, and UP didn't move it further up.
Let me try moving the green square DOWN to see if it goes back into the container body.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Try moving the green square DOWN
print("Trying DOWN...")
result = action(['DOWN'])
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'N':
        print(f"Green after DOWN: boundary={n['boundary']}")
        break

# Try UP
print("\nTrying UP...")
result = action(['UP'])
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'N':
        print(f"Green after UP: boundary={n['boundary']}")
        break

# Try LEFT
print("\nTrying LEFT...")
result = action(['LEFT'])
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'N':
        print(f"Green after LEFT: boundary={n['boundary']}")
        break

# Check full ASCII around green
ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("\nCurrent state (rows 36-45, cols 6-16):")
for i in range(36, 45):
    print(f"  {i}: {ascii_lines[i][6:17]}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
